fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity washington, how to transfer dietitian license to washington, dietitian compact washington, washington dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Dietitian and Nutritionist Credentialing Program
board.mailing_address: Washington State Department of Health, PO Box 47877, Olympia, WA 98504-7877

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian (CD)
license_types.description: The standard state credential to practice dietetics in Washington. Washington uses 'certification' rather than 'licensure' but the credential is legally required to use the title 'dietitian.'

license_types.title: Certified Nutritionist (CN)
license_types.description: A parallel credential for nutrition professionals who meet the state's education and experience requirements but may follow a different national certification pathway.

steps.title: Create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) Account
steps.description: Navigate to secureaccess.wa.gov and create a new SAW account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Add PLQA Service to SAW
steps.description: Log in to your SAW account, then add the Department of Health’s Professional Licensing and Quality Assurance (PLQA) service to access the licensing application system.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Access the PLQA system through your SAW account and complete the online Dietitian application form.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Provide official transcripts verifying a bachelor's or higher degree and verification of your registration as a dietitian from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR). These can often be uploaded directly to the PLQA system or sent electronically/by mail as instructed.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $90 through the online PLQA system.

steps.title: Application Review
steps.description: Monitor your application status online through the PLQA system. The Department of Health will review your application and notify you if additional information is required.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from the educational institution to the Washington DOH.
Proof of current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Documentation of at least 4 hours of approved HIV/AIDS training.
If you hold credentials in other states, verification must be sent directly from each state's licensing authority.

provisional_text: Washington State does not offer a provisional or temporary license for dietitians. Applicants must complete all requirements, including the CDR exam, before certification is granted.
reciprocity_text: Washington allows certification without examination for dietitians licensed or certified in another jurisdiction, provided the DOH determines that the other state's requirements are equivalent to or exceed Washington's. Applicants must provide a letter from their previous state confirming no outstanding disciplinary orders, plus verification of current CDR registration status.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington dietitian certification cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $81 ($65 application + $16 mandatory HEAL-WA surcharge). Annual renewal is $61 ($45 renewal + $16 HEAL-WA surcharge).

faqs.question: Are there continuing education requirements for Washington dietitians?
faqs.answer: Washington State does not require continuing education for state certification renewal. However, to maintain the CDR registration that is a prerequisite for certification, you must complete 75 continuing professional education units every five years through the CDR.

faqs.question: When does the Washington dietitian certification need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Certification must be renewed annually on or before your birthday. As of May 2025, the DOH no longer sends printed renewal notices; all notifications are electronic through the HELMS portal.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Washington has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Washington allows certification without examination for dietitians licensed or certified in another jurisdiction, provided the DOH determines that the other state's requirements are equivalent to or exceed Washington's. Applicants must provide a letter from their previous state confirming no outstanding disciplinary orders, plus verification of current CDR registration status.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Washington
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian license in Washington, you must submit your application online through the Professional Licensing and Quality Assurance (PLQA) system, accessed via your SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account.

official_links.portal_name: Professional Licensing and Quality Assurance (PLQA) system via SecureAccess Washington (SAW)
official_links.registration_steps: First, create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account at secureaccess.wa.gov. Then, add the Department of Health’s Professional Licensing and Quality Assurance (PLQA) service to your SAW account to access and complete the online application.
